mechanism_narrative: Persistent near-zero federal funds rates compress term premiums and signal prolonged accommodation, exerting downward pressure on long-term yields as investors accept lower compensation for duration risk.
failure_condition: Ten-year yield rises more than 50 basis points over any 20-trading-day window while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25 percent; or rolling rank IC remains below 0.03 for two years.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate below 6.5 percent signals a tightening labor market, elevating inflation expectations and pushing investors to demand higher term premiums on long-term bonds.
failure_condition: After a trigger, 20-day yield change is negative in more than 60 percent of instances over a five-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When both industrial production and consumer prices rise above their 12‑month moving averages, the economy is operating with growing supply and demand, generating inflation concerns that increase long‑term yields.
failure_condition: Rank IC for the joint indicator below 0.05 over any three‑year rolling window; or 20‑day forward yield change is not significantly different from zero (t‑test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a housing bust (HOUST below 800K), an increase in housing starts signals an impending recovery, driving yields higher. In a housing boom (HOUST above 1.5M), rising starts warn of speculative excess, reducing risk appetite and lowering yields. The direction flips across regimes.
regime_hint: Housing market regime: bust (HOUST<800K) or boom (HOUST>1.5M)
failure_condition: In the bust regime, 20‑day yield change is negative when HOUST rises; or in the boom regime, 20‑day yield change is positive when HOUST rises; both cases reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation, measured by a month‑over‑month increase in CPIAUCSL exceeding 0.4 percent, erodes real bond yields and forces nominal yields upward in anticipation of tighter monetary policy.
failure_condition: When triggered, 20‑day yield change is negative in more than half of occurrences over a ten‑year period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in retail sales and industrial production above their three‑month moving averages reflect synchronized demand and supply expansion, typically associated with rising real rates and term premiums that lift yields over the short term.
failure_condition: Five‑trading‑day yield change is negative in more than 50 percent of triggered events; or average yield change is not positive (one‑sided test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate persistently above 6 percent indicates substantial labor market slack, constraining wage and price inflation and thereby keeping nominal long‑term yields depressed over multi‑month horizons.
failure_condition: Over any three‑year rolling window with UNRATE above 6 percent, the incidence of positive 60‑day yield changes exceeds 50 percent; or rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A lift‑off in the federal funds rate above 0.25 percent marks the start of a tightening cycle, prompting market participants to reprice term premiums upward as the central bank removes accommodation, leading to higher longer‑term yields.
failure_condition: After a trigger, the 20‑trading‑day change in the 10‑year yield is negative in more than 50 percent of cases over a five‑year walk‑forward; or the average change is not positive (one‑sided p > 0.05).
